title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Build R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), comparable scope NIPTUtils built entirely Rduckhts/DuckDB rather Rsamtools/Python. package ports two upstream implementations: WisecondorX — copy number variation detection cfDNA (Python/pysam upstream) NIPTeR — fast trisomy prediction via chromosomal fraction Z-scores, NCV scores, regression, chi-squared correction (R/Rsamtools upstream) Design priorities: Exact conformance upstream implementations documented testable. Performance — DuckDB SQL replaces per-read Python loops Rsamtools scans; large BAMs run seconds, minutes. Interoperable file formats — bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED files intermediate layer binning analysis, consumable DuckDB, R, Python, tabix-aware tool. Python runtime dependency — Rduckhts replaces pysam HTS operations; reticulate condathis optional used conformance testing.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Shared binning engine — R/convert.R - bam_convert(): DuckDB/SQL read-counting core. Supports binsize, mapq, require_flags (samtools -f), exclude_flags (samtools -F), rmdup ("streaming" / "flag" / "none"), separate_strands (returns list forward/reverse data frames), CRAM via reference. Achieves exact bin--bin conformance WisecondorX HG00106.chrom11 (25,115 non-zero bins, 0 mismatches). - bam_convert_bed(): bgzipped + tabix-indexed 4-column BED output (chrom, start, end, count). Uses Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() rduckhts_tabix_index() — external tools. WisecondorX layer - R/wisecondorx_cli.R: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() — thin condathis wrappers delegating official bioconda package. - R/npz.R: bam_convert_npz() — WisecondorX-compatible NPZ output via reticulate. NIPTeR binning layer — R/nipter_bin.R - nipter_bin_bam(): produces NIPTeRSample objects. separate_strands = FALSE (default), class c("NIPTeRSample", "CombinedStrands"); separate_strands = TRUE, class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands") autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices (forward/reverse, rownames "1F".."22F" / "1R".."22R"). Exposes mapq, require_flags, exclude_flags, rmdup pre-filtering matching real-world NIPT pipelines (e.g. mapq=40L, exclude_flags=1024L). - nipter_bin_bam_bed(): BED output separate_strands support. FALSE (default): 5-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count). TRUE: 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev). Tests - inst/tinytest/test_fixtures.R (41 assertions): synthetic BAM/CRAM fixtures, three rmdup modes, CRAM reference round-trip. - inst/tinytest/test_nipter.R (47 assertions): NIPTeRSample structure, matrix dimensions rownames, MAPQ filter, exclude_flags=1024 dedup, BED.gz output shape, optional bin--bin conformance NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() via NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM env var. - inst/tinytest/test_cli_args.R: CLI argument builder unit tests. - inst/tinytest/test_npz.R: NPZ round-trip tests. - inst/tinytest/test_integration.R: WisecondorX bin--bin conformance via condathis. - inst/tinytest/test_sra_metadata.R: SRA URL helpers. Infrastructure - R/aaa.R: SRA metadata helpers. - scripts/make_fixtures.sh: reproducible synthetic BAM/CRAM fixture generation. - DESCRIPTION: NIPTeR authors (Dirk de Weerd, Lennart Johansson) listed cph. NIPTeR added Suggests. NIPTeR statistical layer file strictly separate; never mix NIPTeR WisecondorX code. statistical functions support CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands samples. R/nipter_control.R — nipter_as_control_group(): constructs NIPTeRControlGroup list NIPTeRSample objects validation dedup. nipter_diagnose_control_group(): per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality tests across control group. nipter_match_control_group(): selects best-fit control samples test sample; distance computation Rcpp+OpenMP nipter_ssd_scores_cpp() kernel (one query vs N columns pre-extracted 22×N fractions matrix). nipter_match_matrix(): computes full N×N pairwise SSD matrix via nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp() — replaces production lapply(1:N, match_control_group(..., mode="report")) hot loop single vectorized call. nipter_control_group_from_beds(): loads directory TSV.bgz files control group one DuckDB pass via rduckhts_tabix_multi(). Internal helpers .sample_chr_fractions() (44-element SeparatedStrands, 22 Combined) .sample_chr_fractions_collapsed() (always 22-element) handle strand dispatch. R/nipter_gc.R — nipter_gc_precompute(): runs rduckhts_fasta_nuc() writes 5-column TSV.bgz+tbi (chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len); use pay FASTA scan cost per reference build rather per sample. nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS bin-weight GC correction accepting gc_table parameter — either path precomputed TSV.bgz, -memory list, NULL (falls back fasta path). correcting NIPTeRControlGroup, GC table resolved reused every sample. SeparatedStrands: LOESS fitted Reduce("+", auto_list) summed counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix via lapply(). Sex chromosome correction via nearest-neighbour (LOESS) -bucket weights (bin-weight). R/nipter_chi.R — nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared overdispersion correction applied simultaneously sample control group. SeparatedStrands: chi computed summed strand counts, correction applied per-strand via lapply(). R/nipter_score.R — nipter_z_score(): chromosomal fraction Z-score Shapiro-Wilk normality test. Uses collapsed fractions SeparatedStrands. nipter_ncv_score(): normalised chromosome value brute-force denominator search using utils::combn() (replaces sets::set_combn() dependency). R/nipter_regression.R — nipter_regression(): forward stepwise regression Z-score train/test split, practical vs theoretical CV selection. Supports CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands. SeparatedStrands doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F","1R".."22R") complementary exclusion (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R" model). R/nipter_sex.R — nipter_sex_model(): 2-component GMM sex prediction via mclust::Mclust(), supporting "y_fraction" "xy_fraction" methods. nipter_sex_model_y_unique(): 2-component GMM Y-unique region read ratios (BAM-level, uses nipter_y_unique_ratio()). nipter_y_unique_ratio(): counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions via DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries returns ratio total nuclear reads. nipter_predict_sex(): classifies sample male/female using one models majority-vote consensus; y_unique_ratio parameter enables Y-unique model vote. Internal .mclust_fit() wrapper evaluates mclust namespace work around mclustBIC() namespace-qualified upstream mclust. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_stats.R — 105 assertions covering statistical functions including SeparatedStrands variants. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_sex.R — 45 assertions covering sex model building, Y-unique model, 3-model consensus, classification accuracy, prediction, edge cases. Native WisecondorX implementation (newref + predict) Pure R/Rcpp port WisecondorX newref predict pipelines, performance-critical KNN reference-finding compiled C++. Python runtime dependency. R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — Shared utilities: .train_gender_model() (2-component GMM Y-fractions zero-variance fallback), .gender_correct(), .get_mask() (5% median coverage threshold), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca() (5 components, ratio correction), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). Also exports scale_sample() rescaling bin sizes. R/rwisecondorx_newref.R — rwisecondorx_newref(): reference building pipeline. Gender model training → global bin mask → per-partition (/F/M) normalize/PCA/distance-filter/KNN/null-ratios. .build_sub_reference() orchestrates partition. .get_reference() delegates Rcpp. R/rwisecondorx_predict.R — rwisecondorx_predict(): prediction pipeline. Rescale → predict gender → normalize autosomes (coverage + PCA + weights + optimal cutoff + 3-pass within-sample normalization aberration masking) → normalize gonosomes → combine → inflate → log-transform → blacklist → CBS → segment Z-scores → aberration calling. .normalize(), .normalize_repeat(), .normalize_once() implement multi-pass normalization. Global--local index conversion .normalize_once() handles index-space mapping correctly. R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs(): CBS wrapper; parallel = TRUE (now default) uses ParDNAcopy::parSegment(num.cores = cpus) explicit thread count; falls back DNAcopy::segment() message ParDNAcopy absent. Matches upstream conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). .get_segment_zscores(): segment-level Z-scores null ratio distributions. R/rwisecondorx_output.R — BED statistics output writers WisecondorXPrediction objects. src/knn_reference.cpp — knn_reference_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized KNN reference bin finding (leave-one-chromosome-squared Euclidean distance, partial sort K nearest). Stores global 1-based indexes. null_ratios_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized null ratio computation using global indexes directly full sample vectors. src/nipter_matching.cpp — nipter_ssd_scores_cpp(): one query vs N columns fractions matrix, returns N SSD scores (OpenMP). nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp(): symmetric N×N pairwise SSD matrix (OpenMP, symmetric schedule). accept cpus thread count. src/RcppExports.cpp, R/RcppExports.R — auto-generated Rcpp glue. R/zzz_rcpp.R — @useDynLib RWisecondorX, .registration = TRUE @importFrom Rcpp sourceCpp roxygen directives. src/Makevars, src/Makevars.win — OpenMP compilation flags. Synthetic cohort generator R/cohort.R — generate_cohort(): creates synthetic BAMs “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin) testing. Produces ~435KB BAMs per sample. Supports trisomy signal injection. Exports COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper batch cohort generation. Tests — native WisecondorX pipeline inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions: reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes distances, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, Z-scores, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity (T21/T18/T13 detected gains, euploid negative control clean). inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions: end--end pipeline (cohort generation → binning → newref → predict → aberration detection). test files use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks. BED.gz reader functions — round-trip stored BED files R/bed_reader.R — bed_to_sample(): reads 4-column BED.gz (bam_convert_bed()) named-list--integer-vectors format rwisecondorx_newref() / rwisecondorx_predict(). bed_to_nipter_sample(): reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) BED.gz (nipter_bin_bam_bed()) NIPTeRSample object. Auto-detects column count via read_tabix() probe; handles literal “NA” corrected_count via TRY_CAST. Per-strand corrected values (corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) read independently SeparatedStrands. inst/tinytest/test_bed_reader.R — 46 assertions covering WisecondorX NIPTeR round-trips, SeparatedStrands 9-column BED, corrected per-strand round-trip, sample name inference, scale_sample() integration.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR layer Sex-stratified NCV X/Y chromosomes: user’s clinical pipeline computes sex-stratified NCV denominators X Y (separate models males vs females). yet implemented. Sex-stratified regression X/Y: Forward stepwise lm() models X Y fractions, stratified predicted sex. yet implemented. Multi-chromosome NIPTeR conformance fixture: make fixtures currently produces chr11-BAM. multi-chromosome synthetic BAM (24 chroms, unmapped reads, -position collisions) allow NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM populated automatically CI without real patient BAM. Native WisecondorX layer — remains pipeline (newref + predict) functionally complete 76 unit tests + 31 cohort pipeline tests pass. remaining work validation extension: End--end conformance vs upstream Python: Bin--bin comparison rwisecondorx_newref() + rwisecondorx_predict() output official Python wisecondorx newref + predict real multi-sample BAMs. reference object (PCA components, KNN indexes, null ratios) final aberration calls need match. Requires controlled multi-sample BAM set bioconda wisecondorx package via condathis. yet automated. WisecondorX reference interoperable file: WisecondorXReference object currently serialized RDS file. matrices (PCA components, mask, indexes, distances, null_ratios) dense numeric arrays don’t map naturally flat BED/TSV. structured HDF5 multiple-TSV.bgz serialization allow Python tooling read reference. priority unless user Python consumer. rwisecondorx_newref() multi-file input via tabix_multi: Currently takes list -memory sample lists. accept bed_dir argument using rduckhts_tabix_multi() load WisecondorX 4-column BEDs , analogous nipter_control_group_from_beds(). Threading budget propagation: rwisecondorx_newref() accepts cpus KNN finding. rwisecondorx_predict() now accepts cpus passes parSegment(). wired together coherently called pipeline script — use cpus value throughout. Beta-mode aberration calling: rwisecondorx_predict() supports zscore beta (purity-based) cutoffs. beta path (ratio > 2^beta) implemented yet tested real data.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: “BED.gz” files BGZF-compressed, tabix-indexed TSV files use BED coordinate conventions (0-based half-open intervals, chr prefix). strict BED files — 3 columns extra columns standard BED fields. Always read read_tabix() (DuckDB SQL) Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_multi(), never read.table() BED-aware parsers assume 3-column BED semantics. 4-column WisecondorX TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, count (written bam_convert_bed()). 5-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (CombinedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()). 9-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (SeparatedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev (written nipter_bin_bam_bed(separate_strands = TRUE)). count = count_fwd + count_rev; corrected_* columns NA GC-corrected sample supplied. 5-column GC table TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len (written nipter_gc_precompute()). Precompute per reference genome + bin size, pass path nipter_gc_correct(gc_table = ...). files bgzipped (BGZF) tabix-indexed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(). use gzfile() external tools. bed_to_sample() reads 4-column TSV.bgz back WisecondorX -memory format. bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5- 9-column TSV.bgz back NIPTeRSample. nipter_control_group_from_beds() loads whole directory via rduckhts_tabix_multi() one DuckDB pass. reading uses SQL read_tabix() table function — never read_bed(). WisecondorX reference (WisecondorXReference) still RDS-. matrices (PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios) dense numeric arrays sensibly represented flat TSV. Use saveRDS() / readRDS().

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: WisecondorX convert step uses streaming dedup state machine (larp/larp2). exact SQL equivalent uses DuckDB window functions FILE_OFFSET: Key subtleties reproduced exactly: Improper pairs (is_paired is_proper) invisible larp larp2. filter internal rmdup = "streaming" — intrinsic WisecondorX algorithm, user flag option. Unpaired reads update larp larp2 — hence IGNORE NULLS trick. larp never reset chromosomes — hence PARTITION . Bin assignment uses integer division pos // binsize matching Python’s int(pos / binsize). pos 0-based: subtract 1 duckhts 1-based POS dividing.
code: LAG(pos) OVER (ORDER BY file_offset)                                          -- prev_pos (larp) LAST_VALUE(CASE WHEN is_paired != 0 THEN pnext END IGNORE NULLS)              -- prev_pnext (larp2)     OVER (ORDER BY file_offset ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: bam_convert(), bam_convert_bed(), nipter_bin_bam(), nipter_bin_bam_bed() expose samtools-style -f/-F flag filtering: require_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & require_flags) == require_flags kept. Equivalent samtools view -f. exclude_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. Equivalent samtools view -F. Common real-world NIPT pre-filter pattern (matches samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024):
code: nipter_bin_bam("sample.dm.bam", mapq = 40L, exclude_flags = 1024L)

previous_headings: NIPTeR Layer: Known Issues and Design Decisions
what: NIPTeR’s scanBam approach is “concat and pretend single-ended”
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_bam_sample() calls scanBam() without filtering parameters, splits read records strand (+ / -), discarding reads strand "*" (unmapped). applies unique() positions per chromosome per strand sums forward reverse counts single matrix. consequence paired-end data treated concatenated mates single-ended read pool: R1 (forward) R2 (reverse) counted independently respective start positions. paired-end library produces roughly 2× bin counts single-end library sequencing depth, mates every fragment counted. creates fundamental incompatibility: NIPTeR reference (control group) built single-end samples used test paired-end sample, vice versa. practice goes unnoticed samples cohort use library preparation, mixing single-end paired-end data produces wildly incorrect Z-scores NCV values. nipter_bin_bam() replicates behaviour (mates counted) default (rmdup = "none", flag filters), keeping compatible NIPTeR control groups built way. Users need fragment-level counts (one count per fragment, per read) pre-filter R1 using require_flags = 0x41L (paired + read1) count unique fragments — future enhancement.

what: NIPTeR’s implicit positional dedup is similar to WisecondorX streaming dedup, but different
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_reads() applies unique() read positions per chromosome per strand binning. effect two reads start position chromosome strand counted , twice. position-based deduplication. WisecondorX’s larp also collapses consecutive reads position. critical differences: collapse -position reads, NIPTeR’s unique() also silently merges two reads happen share start position, including distinct reads PCR duplicates. hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam fixture (1,018 paired-end reads) NIPTeR’s approach count 1,003 vs 1,018 rmdup = "none" — difference 15 reads, positions duplicate flag (0x400) set one mate.

what: Two additional bugs in NIPTeR’s scanBam call
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Bug 1 — Split length mismatch BAMs unmapped reads. strands[strands != "*"] length N − U (U = unmapped reads) reads length N. R’s split() requires equal lengths, errors silently misbehaves BAM containing unmapped reads. NIPTeR implicitly requires samtools view -F 4 pre-filtering. Bug 2 — Positional dedup via unique() flag-ignorant. tabulate() counts unique positions per bin, reads. PCR duplicate unique position survives; two distinct reads position silently merged. equivalent -F 1024. Consequence conformance testing. Exact bin--bin match NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() requires BAM unmapped reads two reads sharing start position per strand. NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM env var must point BAM. rmdup = "none" closest mode counts read independently rather unique positions.
code: # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R splitted_reads <- split(x = reads, f = droplevels(strands[strands != "*"])) # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R / bin_reads() reads <- sapply(X = unique(reads_data_frame[min_read:max_read]),                 FUN = getbins, bin_size = bin_size) bins <- tabulate(reads, nbins = n_bins)

what: KNN Index Semantics — Critical Design Note
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Rcpp implementation (knn_reference_cpp()) stores global 1-based indexes full masked-bin array. Upstream Python (get_ref_for_bins()) stores local indexes relative chr_data (leave-one-chromosome-subset). consequence: global--local conversion .normalize_once() : - Global index g < chr_start → local g (excised chromosome, unchanged) - Global index g > chr_cum → local g - n_chr (excised chromosome, shifted ) - Global index within [chr_start, chr_cum] never occur (-chromosome bins excluded KNN building) change index storage convention without updating .normalize_once() predict null_ratios_cpp() newref.

title: Background
text: extensively comparing different (shallow) whole-genome sequencing-based copy number detection tools, including WISECONDOR, QDNAseq, CNVkit, Control-FREEC, BIC-seq2 cn.MOPS, WISECONDOR appeared normalize sequencing data consistent way, shown paper. Nevertheless, WISECONDOR limitations: Stouffer’s Z-score approach error-prone dealing large amounts aberrations, algorithm extremely slow (24h) operating small bin sizes (15 kb), sex chromosomes part analysis. , present WisecondorX, evolved WISECONDOR aims dealing previous difficulties, resulting overall superior results significantly lower computing times, allowing daily diagnostic use. WisecondorX meant applicable NIPT, also gDNA, PGT, FFPE, LQB, … etc.

title: Background
text: understand underlying algorithm, highly recommend reading Straver et al (2014); update shortly introduced Huijsdens-van Amsterdam et al (2018). Numerous adaptations algorithm made, yet central normalization principles remain. Changes include e.g. inclusion gender prediction algorithm, gender handling prior normalization (ultimately enabling X Y predictions), -sample Z-scoring, inclusion weighted circular binary segmentation algorithm improved codes obtaining tables plots.

title: Background
text: Every dot represents bin. dots range across X-axis chromosome 1 X (Y, case male). vertical position dot represents ratio observed number reads expected number reads, latter ‘normal’ state. values log2-transformed, copy neutral dots close-0. Importantly, notice dots always subject Gaussian noise. Therefore, segments, indicated horizontal white lines, cover bins predicted equal copy number. size dots represents variability reference set. Thus, size increases certainty observation. goes line width segments. Vertical grey bars represent blacklist, matches mostly hypervariable loci repeats. Finally, horizontal colored dotted lines show constitutional 1n 3n states expected (constitutional DNA 100% purity). Often, aberration reach limits, several potential causes: depending type analysis, sample subject tumor fraction, fetal fraction, mosaicism, … etc. Sometimes, segments surpass limits: ’s likely dealing 0n, 4n, 5n, 6n, …

what: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), built top Rduckhts DuckDB. ports WisecondorX (CNV detection) NIPTeR (trisomy prediction via Z-scores, NCV, regression, chi-squared correction), following rewrites.bio principles: exact emulation upstream outputs, full credit original authors, transparency AI assistance. core convert step runs entirely R/SQL via Rduckhts Python dependency. bam_convert() returns per-bin read counts memory; bam_convert_bed() writes bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED file readable DuckDB tabix-aware tool; bam_convert_npz() serialises WisecondorX-compatible .npz via reticulate. convert implementation exactly replicates upstream larp / larp2 streaming deduplication behaviour, achieving bin--bin agreement Python implementation. NIPTeR statistical layer provides GC correction (via --fly FASTA computation, bundled tables), chi-squared overdispersion correction, chromosomal fraction Z-scores, normalised chromosome values (NCV), forward stepwise regression — operating NIPTeRSample objects produced nipter_bin_bam(). pipelines need full upstream toolchain, thin condathis wrappers cover every stage: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() delegate official bioconda package without requiring manual conda setup.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: Install development version GitHub: RWisecondorX imports Rduckhts, DBI, duckdb. reticulate needed write .npz files; condathis needed upstream CLI wrappers. Neither required core convert step.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("sounkou-bioinfo/RWisecondorX")

what: Convert A BAM To Bin Counts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: package ships small chromosome 11 BAM fixture examples tests. rmdup argument controls duplicate handling. "streaming" exactly replicates upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 state machine default. "none" skips deduplication entirely, matching wisecondorx convert --normdup NIPT data read depth low. "flag" uses SAM duplicate flag (0x400) BAMs already processed Picard sambamba.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  fixture_bam <- system.file(   "extdata",   "hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam",   package = "RWisecondorX" )  bins <- bam_convert(   fixture_bam,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup = "streaming" )  names(Filter(Negate(is.null), bins)) #> [1] "11"  length(bins[["11"]]) #> [1] 1198  sum(bins[["11"]]) #> [1] 982

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: bam_convert_bed() language-agnostic output path. produces bgzipped BED file (chrom, start, end, count; 0-based coordinates) tabix index alongside , using rduckhts_bgzip rduckhts_tabix_index Rduckhts — external tools Python required. resulting file can queried region directly DuckDB.
code: bam_convert_bed(   bam     = "sample.bam",   bed     = "sample.bed.gz",   # → sample.bed.gz + sample.bed.gz.tbi   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup   = "streaming" )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: bin counts written BED.gz, bed_to_sample() bed_to_nipter_sample() read back -memory formats expected analysis pipelines. means can bin , store compact BED.gz files, reload number downstream analyses without touching original BAM . bed_to_sample() reads 4-column WisecondorX BED.gz named list integer vectors consumed rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict(): bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) NIPTeR BED.gz NIPTeRSample object compatible NIPTeR statistical functions. Column count auto-detected: typical production workflow bins BAMs stores results, loads analysis across multiple sessions:
code: sample <- bed_to_sample("sample.bed.gz", binsize = 5000L) ns <- bed_to_nipter_sample("sample_nipter.bed.gz", binsize = 50000L) class(ns)  # "NIPTeRSample" "CombinedStrands" library(RWisecondorX)  bam_files <- list.files("bams/", "\\.bam$", full.names = TRUE) bed_dir   <- "bed_counts/" dir.create(bed_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  for (bam in bam_files) {   bed <- file.path(bed_dir, sub("\\.bam$", ".bed.gz", basename(bam)))   bam_convert_bed(bam, bed, binsize = 5000L, rmdup = "streaming") }  bed_files <- list.files(bed_dir, "\\.bed\\.gz$", full.names = TRUE) samples   <- lapply(bed_files, bed_to_sample, binsize = 5000L)  ref <- rwisecondorx_newref(samples, binsize = 100000L, nipt = TRUE, cpus = 4L) saveRDS(ref, "reference.rds")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: full upstream WisecondorX pipeline needed, wisecondorx_convert() wraps wisecondorx convert via condathis produces .npz official Python implementation. wisecondorx_newref() wisecondorx_predict() cover remaining stages. three handle conda environment creation automatically first use. CLI wrappers expose upstream arguments documented WisecondorX README. delegate official wisecondorx bioconda package installed automatically via condathis.
code: wisecondorx_convert(   bam     = "sample.bam",   npz     = "sample.npz",   binsize = 5000L,   normdup = FALSE          # TRUE → --normdup (NIPT) ) library(RWisecondorX)  fixture_npz <- tempfile(fileext = ".npz") np <- reticulate::import("numpy", convert = FALSE)  bam_convert_npz(   bam     = fixture_bam,   npz     = fixture_npz,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup   = "streaming",   np      = np )  c(   npz_created = file.exists(fixture_npz),   npz_size    = file.info(fixture_npz)$size ) #> npz_created    npz_size  #>           1         392 library(RWisecondorX)  npz_files <- list.files("controls/", "\\.npz$", full.names = TRUE)  wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files   = npz_files,   output      = "reference.npz",   ref_binsize = 100000L,   nipt        = TRUE,   refsize     = 300L,   plotyfrac   = "yfrac_plot.png",   cpus        = 4L )  wisecondorx_predict(   npz             = "sample.npz",   ref             = "reference.npz",   output_prefix   = "results/sample",   bed             = TRUE,   plot            = TRUE,   add_plot_title  = TRUE,   seed            = 1L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict() pure R/Rcpp implementations full WisecondorX algorithm — reference building, PCA correction, within-sample normalisation, CBS segmentation, aberration calling — Python dependency. Performance-critical KNN reference-bin finding compiled via Rcpp OpenMP parallelisation. CBS segmentation uses DNAcopy (ParDNAcopy parallel operation) directly instead calling upstream CBS.R subprocess. typical workflow : bin samples bam_convert(), build reference rwisecondorx_newref(), call aberrations rwisecondorx_predict(). reference plain R list can serialised saveRDS() shared sessions.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  bam_files <- list.files("controls/", "\\.bam$", full.names = TRUE)  samples <- lapply(bam_files, bam_convert, binsize = 5000L, rmdup = "streaming")  ref <- rwisecondorx_newref(   samples  = samples,   binsize  = 100000L,   nipt     = TRUE,   refsize  = 300L,   cpus     = 4L )  saveRDS(ref, "reference.rds")  test_sample <- bam_convert("test.bam", binsize = 5000L, rmdup = "streaming")  prediction <- rwisecondorx_predict(   sample         = test_sample,   reference      = ref,   sample_binsize = 5000L,   outprefix      = "results/test",   zscore         = 5,   alpha          = 1e-4,   seed           = 1L )  prediction$aberrations prediction$statistics

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: generate_cohort() creates synthetic BAM cohort suitable end--end pipeline testing. produces 50 samples using “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per 100kb genomic bin), BAM ~435KB. cohort includes euploid females males plus three trisomy samples (T21, T18, T13) sensitivity validation.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  out_dir <- tempfile("cohort_") generate_cohort(out_dir)  bams <- list.files(out_dir, "\\.bam$", full.names = TRUE) length(bams)  # 50  samples <- lapply(bams, bam_convert,                   binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE, rmdup = "none") ref <- rwisecondorx_newref(samples, binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE,                            nipt = TRUE, cpus = 4L)  t21_bam <- file.path(out_dir, "sample_048_T21.bam") t21 <- bam_convert(t21_bam, binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE, rmdup = "none") pred <- rwisecondorx_predict(t21, ref,                              sample_binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE,                              zscore = 5, seed = 42L)  pred$aberrations[pred$aberrations$type == "gain", c("chr", "start", "end", "zscore")]

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_sex_model() trains 2-component Gaussian mixture model chromosome fraction data control group sex prediction. Three model types available: Y-fraction alone, bivariate X+Y fractions, Y-unique region read ratios specific Y-chromosome genes. nipter_predict_sex() classifies test sample using one models majority-vote consensus.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, fasta = "hg38.fa") corrected <- nipter_chi_correct(samples[[1]], cg) cg <- corrected$control_group  model_y  <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "y_fraction") model_xy <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "xy_fraction")  nipter_predict_sex(test_sample, models = list(model_y, model_xy))

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX also ports statistical analysis layer NIPTeR (de Weerd et al.), replacing Rsamtools Rduckhts bundled sysdata.rda tables --fly computation via rduckhts_fasta_nuc(). analysis pipeline follows NIPTeR’s workflow: bin samples, build control group, correct GC bias overdispersion, compute Z-scores NCV values trisomy prediction.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_bin_bam() produces NIPTeRSample objects compatible downstream functions. Pre-filtering flags mirror samtools view -f/-F conventions, real-world NIPT pipelines mark duplicates Picard can pass exclude_flags = 1024L directly.
code: samples <- lapply(bam_files, nipter_bin_bam, binsize = 50000L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: control group reference cohort test samples scored. nipter_as_control_group() validates deduplicates input samples. nipter_diagnose_control_group() reports per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality p-values across group, useful identifying outlier samples scoring. working large cohort, nipter_match_control_group() selects closest-matching subset given test sample based chromosomal fraction distance.
code: cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) diag <- nipter_diagnose_control_group(cg) matched_cg <- nipter_match_control_group(test_sample, cg, n_controls = 50L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_gc_correct() adjusts bin counts GC-content bias. GC percentages computed per bin reference FASTA via rduckhts_fasta_nuc() rather static bundled tables. Two methods available: LOESS regression (default, matching NIPTeR) bin-weight normalisation. function accepts either single sample entire control group.
code: cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, fasta = "hg38.fa", method = "loess") test_sample <- nipter_gc_correct(test_sample, fasta = "hg38.fa")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_chi_correct() identifies overdispersed bins across control group using normalised chi-squared test downweights . correction applied simultaneously test sample control group, maintaining consistency downstream scoring.
code: corrected <- nipter_chi_correct(test_sample, cg, chi_cutoff = 3.5) test_sample <- corrected$sample cg <- corrected$control_group

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_z_score() computes chromosomal fraction Z-score focus chromosome, testing far sample deviates control distribution. Z-score 3 conventional threshold trisomy. result includes Shapiro-Wilk p-value control distribution normality violations visible. nipter_ncv_score() computes normalised chromosome value, searches denominator chromosome set minimises control-group variance computing Z-score. robust standard Z-score control group small chromosomal fraction high variance. nipter_regression() uses forward stepwise linear regression predict focus chromosome’s fraction chromosomes, scores residual. builds multiple models train/test split selects CV (practical vs theoretical) gives conservative estimate.
code: z21 <- nipter_z_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) z21$sample_z_score z21$control_statistics ncv21 <- nipter_ncv_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) ncv21$sample_ncv_score ncv21$denominators reg21 <- nipter_regression(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) reg21$models[[1]]$z_score reg21$models[[1]]$predictors

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: bam_convert(   bam,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 5000L (WisecondorX); use 50000L NIPTeR-style workflows. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 1L (WisecondorX / samtools default). Set 0L retain reads regardless MAPQ (NIPTeR). require_flags Integer bitmask. reads (FLAG & require_flags) == require_flags retained. 0L (default) imposes requirement. Example: require_flags = 0x2L keeps properly paired reads. exclude_flags Integer bitmask. Reads (FLAG & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. 0L (default) drops nothing. Example: exclude_flags = 0xF04L drops unmapped, secondary, QC-fail supplementary reads (common samtools pre-filter). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "streaming" (default) applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 algorithm (also excludes improper pairs). "flag" drops reads SAM flag 0x400. "none" keeps reads pass filters. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, returns per-strand counts (forward + reverse -). return value changes list two named lists (fwd rev), structured like default return. Used nipter_bin_bam(separate_strands = TRUE) NIPTeR SeparatedStrands object. Default FALSE. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default) temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: separate_strands = FALSE (default): named list one integer vector per chromosome key ("1"–"22", "23" X, "24" Y). vector contains per-bin read counts (bin 0 = positions 0 binsize - 1). Chromosomes absent BAM NULL. separate_strands = TRUE: list two elements, fwd rev, structured like default return.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Read filtering mirrors samtools view convention: mapq sets minimum mapping quality; require_flags bitmask flags must set (equivalent samtools view -f); exclude_flags bitmask flags must clear (equivalent samtools view -F).  Use duckhts UDF sam_flag_bits(flag) inspect named flag fields, sam_flag_has(flag, bit) test individual bits. rmdup controls duplicate removal independently flag filters: "streaming" applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 consecutive-position state machine also enforces WisecondorX improper-pair rule (paired reads properly paired excluded counting dedup state — intrinsic algorithm, flag option); "flag" additionally excludes reads SAM flag 0x400 set (Picard / sambamba pre-marked duplicates); "none" applies deduplication.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # WisecondorX defaults bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam")  # NIPTeR defaults — all mapped reads, no dedup, 50 kb bins bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L, mapq = 0L,                     rmdup = "none")  # Pre-filtered BAM: skip unmapped + secondary + supplementary, flag dedup bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam",                     exclude_flags = bitwOr(0x4L, bitwOr(0x100L, 0x800L)),                     rmdup = "flag") } # }

text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file (created overwritten). tabix index written paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 1L). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L (nothing dropped). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default), temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; TRUE (default) tabix index created alongside bgzipped output.

what: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: Replicates NIPTeR's bin_bam_sample(): counts reads fixed-width bins across chromosomes 1-22, X, Y. original NIPTeR counts mapped reads MAPQ filter; real-world NIPT pipelines typically pre-filter samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024 binning. modes supported mapq, exclude_flags, require_flags parameters.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: nipter_bin_bam(   bam,   binsize = 50000L,   mapq = 0L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 50000 (NIPTeR's fixed bin size). Must match binsize used building control group. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 0L (NIPTeR original: mapped reads). Set 40L match common NIPT pipeline pre-filtering (samtools view --min-MQ 40). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L. Set 1024L (0x400) exclude reads marked duplicates (samtools view -F 1024). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "none" (default, NIPTeR standard) "flag" (equivalent exclude_flags = 1024L; BAMs already processed Picard / sambamba). NIPTeR perform streaming deduplication. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, produces SeparatedStrands object independent forward/reverse count matrices. Default FALSE (CombinedStrands). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: object class c("NIPTeRSample", <strand_type>): CombinedStrands (default): autosomal_chromosome_reads list one 22-row integer matrix (rows "1"–"22"); sex_chromosome_reads list one 2-row matrix (rows "X", "Y"). SeparatedStrands (separate_strands = TRUE): autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices — element 1 forward (rows "1F"–"22F"), element 2 reverse (rows "1R"–"22R"); sex_chromosome_reads similarly contains forward ("XF", "YF") reverse ("XR", "YR") matrices.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: result reshaped NIPTeRSample object whose structure parallels NIPTeR's NIPTSample: autosomal reads chromosome--bin matrix sex chromosome reads separate two-row matrix. separate_strands = TRUE, forward (+) reverse (-) reads counted independently, producing two matrices per chromosome set (class "SeparatedStrands"). doubles predictor pool nipter_regression() — see NIPTeR documentation details.

what: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: Identifies bins control group excess variance (overdispersion) using chi-squared test downweights . reduces influence noisy bins downstream Z-scores NCV calculations.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: sample NIPTeRSample object (test sample). control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chi_cutoff Normalised chi-squared threshold. Bins (chi - df) / sqrt(2*df) > chi_cutoff corrected. Default 3.5. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes well? Default FALSE. Sex chromosome bins use chi-squared weights derived autosomes.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: list two elements: sample corrected NIPTeRSample. control_group corrected NIPTeRControlGroup.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: correction applied simultaneously test sample control group samples, maintaining consistency. algorithm follows NIPTeR's chi-squared correction: control sample, scale autosomal bin counts total reads match overall mean across control samples. Compute expected count per bin (mean scaled counts). Compute chi-squared per bin: \(\chi^2 = \sum_i (expected - scaled_i)^2 / expected\). Normalise: \(z = (\chi^2 - df) / \sqrt{2 \cdot df}\) \(df = n_{controls} - 1\). bins \(z > \text{chi\_cutoff}\): divide reads \(\chi^2 / df\).

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: object NIPTeRSample NIPTeRControlGroup. fasta Path indexed reference FASTA file (.fa/.fasta .fai index). Ignored gc_table supplied. method GC correction method: "loess" (default) "bin" (bin-weight). span LOESS smoothing parameter (used method = "loess"). Default 0.75. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes (X, Y) well? Default FALSE. binsize Bin size used binning sample (default 50000L). Must match binsize sample. Ignored gc_table list (bin size already encoded vector lengths). gc_table Pre-computed GC table. Either path TSV.bgz file (nipter_gc_precompute) -memory named list returned previous .get_gc_table() call. NULL (default), GC content computed fasta. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts loaded. NULL, temporary connection created.

text: corrected copy object class. Correction status updated "Uncorrected" "GC corrected".

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: GC content can supplied three ways via gc_table parameter: Pre-computed file Path TSV.bgz produced nipter_gc_precompute. Fastest large cohorts — compute , reuse every sample. -memory list Named list numeric vectors (one per chromosome) returned .get_gc_table(). Useful chaining corrections within session. FASTA path via fasta Compute GC --fly every call. Convenient single-sample use; slow many samples. LOESS method (default): Fits LOESS curve read counts vs GC percentage across autosomal bins known GC non-zero reads. bin scaled median(counts) / fitted(loess), bins normalised genome-wide median. NIPTeR default method. Bin-weight method: Groups bins GC percentage (0.1\ computes mean read count per GC bucket, scales bin global_mean / bucket_mean. Faster LOESS less smooth. Sex chromosome correction (include_sex = TRUE) uses nearest-neighbour lookup autosomal LOESS curve (LOESS method) GC bucket weights (bin-weight method).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: Implements NIPTeR's stepwise linear regression approach. Builds multiple regression models predict focus chromosome's fraction chromosomal fractions, computes Z-scores based prediction residuals.

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: sample NIPTeRSample object. control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chromo_focus Integer; target chromosome (1-22). n_models Number regression models build (default 4). n_predictors Maximum number predictor chromosomes per model (default 4). exclude_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes exclude predictor pool (default c(13, 18, 21)). include_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes force-include. train_fraction Fraction control samples used training (default 0.6). overdispersion_rate Overdispersion multiplier theoretical CV (default 1.15). force_practical_cv Logical; always use practical CV regardless theoretical CV? Default FALSE. seed Random seed train/test split. Default NULL (seed set).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: list class "NIPTeRRegression" elements: models list n_models sublists, containing: z_score (numeric), cv (selected CV), cv_type ("practical" "theoretical"), predictors (character vector predictor chromosomes), shapiro_p_value, control_z_scores (named numeric vector). focus_chromosome Character. correction_status Character vector. sample_name Character.

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: SeparatedStrands samples predictor pool doubled (autosomal chromosome contributes forward reverse fraction), complementary-strand exclusion within model prevents selecting strands chromosome predictors model. Across models, exact predictor string excluded; complementary strand remains available. algorithm: Split control group 60/40 train test sets. Compute chromosomal fractions samples. n_models models, greedily select n_predictors chromosomes maximising adjusted R-squared (forward stepwise selection). Predictors already used previous models excluded. model, fit lm(focus ~ predictors) training set, predict test set, compute: Practical CV: sd(obs/pred) / mean(obs/pred) test set. Theoretical CV: overdispersion_rate / sqrt(total_reads_focus) test sample. larger CV used (unless force_practical_cv = TRUE). Sample Z-score: (sample_ratio - 1) / cv.

text: control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. Ideally chi-squared corrected via nipter_chi_correct. method Character; feature space GMM: "y_fraction" Univariate: Y-chromosome read fraction relative total autosomal reads. "xy_fraction" Bivariate: X Y chromosome fractions.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: object class "NIPTeRSexModel" elements: model mclust::Mclust fitted object. method Character; method used. male_cluster Integer (1 2); cluster male (higher Y fraction). classifications Named character vector "male"/ "female" labels control sample. fractions Matrix input features used fitting (samples rows). Columns "y_fraction" c("x_fraction", "y_fraction").

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: mirrors approach used clinical NIPT pipelines (see Details), ported R using mclust::Mclust() place Python's sklearn.GaussianMixture. algorithm: Compute sex chromosome read fractions every sample control group. Y fraction = sum(Y bins) / sum(autosomal bins); X fraction = sum(X bins) / sum(autosomal bins). Fit two-component Gaussian mixture equal mixing proportions (mclust::Mclust(data, G = 2,     control = mclust::emControl(equalPro = TRUE))). Identify male cluster component higher median Y fraction. follows clinical NIPT pipeline pattern building sex models control cohorts. user's pipeline builds three models (Y-unique ratio samtools, XY fractions, Y fraction) takes majority vote; nipter_predict_sex() implements consensus given multiple models.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build sex model from chi-corrected control group sex_model <- nipter_sex_model(chi_cg, method = "y_fraction")  # Bivariate X+Y model sex_model_xy <- nipter_sex_model(chi_cg, method = "xy_fraction") } # }

Default 100000L. sample_binsizes Optional integer vector per-sample bin sizes. text: list (reference object) class "WisecondorXReference", containing autosomal (optionally) gonosomal sub-references. See Details full structure.

title: Build a WisecondorX reference from binned samples — rwisecondorx_newref
text: pipeline trains gender model (2-component GMM Y-fractions), optionally applies gender correction non-NIPT workflows, computes global bin mask, builds three sub-references: autosomes (), female gonosomes (F), male gonosomes (M). sub-reference includes PCA components, within-sample reference bin indices distances, null ratios -sample Z-scoring. faithful port upstream Python wisecondorx newref, crediting original WisecondorX authors. returned reference object contains: binsize Integer; reference bin size. is_nipt Logical; whether NIPT mode used. trained_cutoff Numeric; Y-fraction gender cutoff. has_female Logical; whether female gonosomal reference exists. has_male Logical; whether male gonosomal reference exists. mask, bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr_cum, pca_components, pca_mean, indexes, distances, null_ratios Autosomal reference components. mask.F, ..., null_ratios.F Female gonosomal reference (present). mask.M, ..., null_ratios.M Male gonosomal reference (present).

title: Predict copy number aberrations using WisecondorX — rwisecondorx_predict
text: sample Named list integer vectors keyed chromosome ("1"–"24"), returned bam_convert(). reference WisecondorXReference object rwisecondorx_newref(), list equivalent structure. sample_binsize Integer; bin size input sample. Used rescaling reference bin size. outprefix Character; path prefix output files. BED statistics files written <outprefix>_bins.bed, <outprefix>_segments.bed, <outprefix>_aberrations.bed, <outprefix>_statistics.txt. NULL (default), files written results returned list. minrefbins Integer; minimum reference bins per target. Bins fewer references zeroed . Default 150L. maskrepeats Integer; number iterative distance-masking cycles. Default 5L. alpha Numeric; CBS breakpoint p-value threshold. Default 1e-4. zscore Numeric; Z-score cutoff aberration calling. Default 5. beta Optional numeric; given, ratio-based cutoff used instead Z-score. approximate purity (0, 1]. Default NULL. blacklist Optional character; path headerless BED file regions mask. gender Optional character; force gender ("F" "M"). seed Optional integer; RNG seed CBS reproducibility. parallel Logical; use ParDNAcopy::parSegment() CBS available. Default TRUE. Falls back DNAcopy::segment() message ParDNAcopy installed. cpus Integer; number threads parallel CBS (parSegment) OpenMP-accelerated steps. Default 1L.

what: SeparatedStrands BED expanded to 9 columns; BED readers switched to read_tabix()
title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: nipter_bin_bam_bed(separate_strands = TRUE) now writes 9-column BED files: chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev. previous 7-column format lacked per-strand corrected values, GC-corrected SeparatedStrands samples lost per-strand correction information BED round-trip. bed_to_sample() bed_to_nipter_sample() now use read_tabix() instead read_bed() reading BED.gz files. read_tabix() returns columns VARCHAR BED-schema type coercion, avoiding problem read_bed() maps columns 7-8 thick_start/thick_end (INTEGER) rejects double-precision corrected values. bed_to_nipter_sample() format detection now uses tryCatch() around column5 probe query, correctly falls back CombinedStrands file 5 columns (column5 exist read_tabix). New tests corrected per-strand round-trip: CombinedStrands corrected values SeparatedStrands per-strand corrected values (independent forward/reverse multipliers) survive write→read cycle within floating-point tolerance. Independence forward reverse corrections explicitly verified. Fixed R CMD check NOTEs: utils::globalVariables() mclust symbols R/aaa.R; utils::write.table() qualified R/convert.R R/nipter_bin.R. Total test count: 391 assertions, passing.

what: BED.gz reader functions — close the round-trip
title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: New bed_to_sample() reads 4-column BED.gz file (written bam_convert_bed()) back named-list--integer-vectors format expected rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict(). closes WisecondorX round-trip: bin bam_convert_bed(), store BED.gz, reload analysis without re-reading BAM. New bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) BED.gz file (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()) NIPTeRSample object compatible NIPTeR statistical functions. Column count auto-detected. Handles literal "NA" strings corrected_count field via TRY_CAST. Sample name inferred filename set explicitly. functions accept optional DuckDB connection reuse across multiple files, creating one internally (allow_unsigned_extensions = "true") none supplied. New inst/tinytest/test_bed_reader.R — 46 assertions covering WisecondorX 4-column round-trip, NIPTeR CombinedStrands 5-column round-trip, SeparatedStrands 9-column round-trip (four matrices), corrected per-strand round-trip, sample name inference, integration scale_sample(). Total test count: 391 assertions (46 test_bed_reader.R), passing.

title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: New rwisecondorx_newref() — pure-R/Rcpp implementation WisecondorX newref pipeline. Takes list binned samples (bam_convert()) builds PCA-based reference: gender model training (2-component GMM Y-fractions), global bin masking, per-partition normalize/PCA/filter/KNN reference building, null ratio computation. Supports NIPT mode, custom Y-fraction cutoff, multi-threaded KNN via OpenMP. New rwisecondorx_predict() — pure-R implementation WisecondorX predict pipeline. Coverage normalization, PCA projection, iterative within-sample normalization aberration masking, gonosomal normalization, result inflation, log-transformation, optional blacklist masking, CBS segmentation (via DNAcopy ParDNAcopy), segment Z-scoring, aberration calling. Supports Z-score beta/ratio calling modes. New rwisecondorx_write_bins_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_segments_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_aberrations_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_statistics() writing prediction results BED statistics files. New scale_sample() rescaling binned samples different bin sizes (e.g. 5kb → 100kb). R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — shared utilities including .train_gender_model() (mclust NULL fallback zero-variance Y-fractions -female cohorts), .gender_correct(), .get_mask(), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca(), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs() wrapper around DNAcopy’s segment() upstream WisecondorX conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). Supports ParDNAcopy parallel segmentation.

what: Fix: mclust GMM on zero-variance Y-fractions
title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: .train_gender_model() now handles case female samples exactly 0.0 Y-fraction (Y reads). mclust::Mclust(..., G=2) returns NULL scenario; fix falls back gap-based cutoff: min(nonzero_y_fractions) / 2.

title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: New generate_cohort() creates synthetic BAM files testing using “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin instead 100kb). Produces identical bin COUNT structure GRCh37 100kb resolution ~435KB BAMs. Supports injecting trisomy signal (extra reads target chromosome). New COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L) exported use bam_convert(binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper cohort generation.

title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: test files now use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks load R code directly. required functions call compiled C++ code (knn_reference_cpp, null_ratios_cpp) available installed package. Fixed tinytest assertion: expect_message(..., pattern = NA) mean “expect messages” — fails message emitted. Replaced expect_silent(). New inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions covering reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity. New inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions end--end pipeline: cohort generation → binning → reference building → prediction → trisomy detection. Tests trisomy 21, 18, 13 detected gains euploid samples produce aberrations. Total test count: 345 assertions, passing.

what: Y-unique region ratio for sex prediction
title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: New nipter_y_unique_ratio() counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions (HSFY1, BPY2, BPY2B, BPY2C, XKRY, PRY, PRY2) computes ratio total nuclear genome reads. Uses DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries (read_bam(region := ...)) efficient BAM access. bundled GRCh37 regions file can replaced custom file assemblies. New nipter_sex_model_y_unique() fits 2-component GMM Y-unique ratios (one per BAM cohort), producing NIPTeRSexModel compatible nipter_predict_sex(). nipter_predict_sex() gains y_unique_ratio parameter passing pre-computed Y-unique ratio "y_unique" model included consensus vote. enables full 3-model majority-vote sex prediction pipeline (Y-unique ratio + Y fraction + XY fractions). Bundled inst/extdata/grch37_Y_UniqueRegions.txt — TSV 7 GRCh37 Y-unique regions used Y-unique ratio calculation.

what: Sex prediction via Gaussian mixture models
title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: New nipter_sex_model() fits 2-component GMM sex chromosome fractions NIPTeRControlGroup using mclust::Mclust(). Supports "y_fraction" (univariate Y-chromosome fraction) "xy_fraction" (bivariate X + Y fractions) methods. male cluster identified component higher median Y fraction. New nipter_predict_sex() classifies NIPTeRSample male female given one NIPTeRSexModel objects. Multiple models use majority vote consensus (tie defaults “female” — conservative NIPT). mclust added Suggests DESCRIPTION.

title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: nipter_bin_bam_bed() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, outputs 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) count = count_fwd + count_rev. FALSE (default), 5-column BED format unchanged.

title: RWisecondorX (development version)
text: bam_convert() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, returns list fwd (forward strand) rev (reverse strand) data frames. nipter_bin_bam() gains separate_strands = TRUE support, producing NIPTeRSample objects class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands"). Autosomal chromosome reads stored list two matrices (forward reverse) rownames "1F".."22F" "1R".."22R". NIPTeR statistical functions now support SeparatedStrands samples: nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS/bin-weight fitted summed strand counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix. nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared computed summed strands, correction applied per-strand. nipter_z_score() nipter_ncv_score(): use collapsed (F+R) fractions. nipter_regression(): doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F", "1R".."22R") complementary exclusion within model (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R").
